---
title: "DANL 410 Capstone"
---

## Forecasting NFL Wide Receiver Production from Draft Information

This capstone project studies whether NFL draft position and pre-draft information can predict wide receiver production over a player's first five professional seasons. The analysis uses NFL draft, combine, game, play, passing, and receiving data to build a receiver-level modeling dataset.

[Open Full Capstone Report](wr_draft_capstone_report.html){.btn .btn-primary}

## Project Materials

- **Research question:** How well does draft position predict wide receiver production over the first five NFL seasons, and how much do other pre-draft variables improve that prediction?
- **Dataset:** Drafted NFL wide receivers from 2005 to 2015, matched to first-five-season receiving production.
- **Methods:** Exploratory data analysis, linear models, random forest modeling, holdout evaluation, rolling draft-year cross-validation, residual analysis, and EPA-per-target robustness checks.
- **Outcome focus:** First-five-season receiving yards, with EPA per target used as a robustness outcome.

## Submission Checklist

The full report includes the major capstone components:

- Research question and project framing
- Data sources and dataset construction
- Descriptive statistics and visual exploration
- Predictive modeling and model comparison
- Rolling cross-validation
- Feature importance and coefficient interpretation
- Residual analysis
- Robustness analysis with EPA per target

## Notes

The raw data files and Quarto source file are submitted separately in Brightspace. This page provides the public project webpage link for the capstone report.
